summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, CS education / AI policy, Games / criticism. The strongest items in this run are ‘Uncharted territory’: uncertainty as US vaccine guidance falls apart under Trump, Hungary’s prime minister-elect vows to suspend ‘propaganda machine’ state media, and Oklahoma principal shot disarming ex-student with semi-automatic guns.

mainItems.title: ‘Uncharted territory’: uncertainty as US vaccine guidance falls apart under Trump
mainItems.summary: Several shots – including flu and Covid – lost their CDC recommendations under overhauls from the White House Several shots lost their recommendation from the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) after a judge’s stay against…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Hungary’s prime minister-elect vows to suspend ‘propaganda machine’ state media
mainItems.summary: Péter Magyar compares media coverage to Nazi-era Germany and aims to ‘restore its public service character’ Europe live – latest updates Hungary’s prime minister-elect has vowed to suspend state media news coverage, describing it as a…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Oklahoma principal shot disarming ex-student with semi-automatic guns
mainItems.summary: Kirk Moore was injured as he tackled a former pupil who opened fire at Pauls Valley high school Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox An Oklahoma principal has been praised for preventing a tragedy…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: Leaked GTA Online revenue data boosts publisher Take-Two's stock
mainItems.summary: Even though the hacker group ShinyHunters successfully broke through (part of) Rockstar Games' security and extracted internal data, the developer and its huge publisher, Take-Two, haven't minded the unfortunate event much.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Microsoft faces fresh Windows Recall security concerns
mainItems.summary: When Microsoft tried to launch Recall, an AI-powered Windows feature that screenshots most of what you do on your PC, it was labeled a "disaster" for cybersecurity and a "privacy nightmare.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: With DLSS 5, Nvidia look to rewrite the truth before our eyes
mainItems.summary: Earlier this year Nvidia announced DLSS 5 , a new version of their DLSS technology: a layer of graphical magic that uses their GPUs to do improbable things to the resolution and framerate of your favourite PC games (unless your favourite…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Useful if you care about how games actually work as designed experiences.

mainItems.title: UK gov's Mythos AI tests help separate cybersecurity threat from hype
mainItems.summary: New model is the first AI system to complete a difficult multistep infiltration challenge.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Opinion: Why Colleges, School Districts and Hospitals Are Closing On-Site Child Care
mainItems.summary: In February, the University of Nebraska at Omaha (UNO) announced it would shutter its on-campus child care center, which has operated for nearly 40 years, at the end of the spring semester.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: Emails Reveal Space Force’s Hardest Mission Is Writing a Song
mainItems.summary: Internal Space Force emails obtained by 404 Media show the work it takes to have a government agency make a new theme song. A general even wanted to start the whole thing over again.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: How Toni Morrison blurred the lines between being an editor and a writer
mainItems.summary: Two recent books about Morrison attempt to make sense of her legacy as a writer, editor and thinker on Black life When I think of Toni Morrison’s novels, I often think of the poet Lucille Clifton’s response to Gorilla, My Love, the debut…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: Quit a GLP-1? Plan to start again? It's not recommended, but plenty of people do it
mainItems.summary: A majority of people who start the obesity and diabetes medicines known as GLP-1s also quit them, and plan to restart again. Research hasn't yet shown the health impacts of cycling on and off the drugs.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: Godzilla Minus Zero: First Trailer Sees the Statue of Liberty in Danger
mainItems.summary: And he’s barreling into New York in IMAX, no less The post Godzilla Minus Zero : First Trailer Sees the Statue of Liberty in Danger appeared first on Reactor .
mainItems.whyItMatters: On-theme for your cultural interests, especially when the concept is strong.

mainItems.title: How Silicon Valley Is Turning Scientists Into Exploited Gig Workers
mainItems.summary: Hirsh Chitkara Tech elites are enriching themselves by plundering STEM institutions—and offering researchers scraps. The post How Silicon Valley Is Turning Scientists Into Exploited Gig Workers appeared first on The Nation .
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to systems, infrastructure, and technical risk.

youtube.title: Shipping traffic slows as the U.S., Iran blockade the Strait of Hormuz
youtube.summary: The duel blockades have limited the number of ships passing through the strait to just two in the past day, according to ship-tracking firms and media reports.

youtube.title: Two members of Congress say they’ll resign, following allegations of sexual misconduct
youtube.summary: Two members of Congress say they’ll resign, following allegations of sexual misconduct: Texas Republican Tony Gonzales and California Democrat Eric Swalwell, who's also announced he’s suspending his campaign for governor of California.

trailers.title: Roosters: Season 2 | Official Trailer | Netflix
trailers.summary: The men are back, more confused than ever. Season 2 of Roosters arrives May 13. Watch on Netflix: https://www.netflix.com/title/81698659 In season 2, Mike, Danny, Ivo, and Greg do everything they can to be less cocky.
trailers.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

trailers.title: Get up. Chapter Three of #ImperfectWomen is now streaming.
trailers.summary: Elisabeth Moss, Kerry Washington & Kate Mara are your Imperfect Women, now streaming on Apple TV https://apple.co/_ImperfectWomen A new series starring Elisabeth Moss, Kerry Washington, and Kate Mara.
trailers.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.

highlights.title: A.I. Is Prompting an Evolution, Not Extinction, for Coders
highlights.notes: Interesting piece on the current state of AI in software work.

highlights.title: The Workers Letting A.I. Do Their Jobs
highlights.notes: In an era of agents powered by artificial intelligence, many programmers are barely programming.
